description_long: This object consists of two traditional East Asian pagoda-style buildings, with the larger structure featuring a multi-tiered, dark brown roof with pronounced upward-curving eaves and a decorative finial at the apex. The main building is supported by red columns and has a wide, open base with visible entryways and a prominent staircase at the front. The smaller adjacent structure mirrors the architectural style, with a single-tiered roof, red columns, and similarly upturned eaves. Both buildings display a harmonious color palette of deep brown, red, and subtle gold accents, evoking the aesthetic of historic temple complexes. The roofs are expansive and layered, providing significant overhang and visual complexity. The overall design suggests a ceremonial or religious function, typical of pagodas found in Japanese temple grounds.
description: A pair of traditional Japanese-style pagoda buildings with multi-tiered, dark brown roofs, red columns, and upward-curving eaves, featuring ornate architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main pagoda's wide staircase, entryways, and the smaller adjacent building to the right.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the main pagoda and the side profile of the smaller building, with visible roof curvature.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the layered roofs and the spatial relationship between the two buildings.
description_view_3: Opposite front view with the main pagoda's entryways and the smaller building positioned to the left.
